mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:historical correlation of employment growth and equity returns
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating economic outlook, leading to reduced consumer confidence and corporate profits, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp over 3 months, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 45% of such triggered instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is high and the federal funds rate remains low, it indicates an accommodative monetary policy relative to price pressures, which can fuel asset price appreciation due to negative real rates, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:real interest rates impact equity valuations
failure_condition: If the trigger condition is met, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales growth during periods of economic expansion indicates strong consumer confidence and spending, directly boosting corporate revenues and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:consumer spending is a major GDP component
failure_condition: If RSAFS growth is positive and the economy is in expansion, but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 40% of triggered instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts signals a recovery or strength in the housing market, which has broad economic multiplier effects through construction, employment, and consumer spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average rises by > 5% from previous 3-month average
evidence_refs: fact:housing market is a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: If the HOUST trigger condition is met, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 4-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production indicates increasing manufacturing output and capacity utilization, reflecting strong underlying economic activity and corporate profitability, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.3% for 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:industrial production reflects business cycle strength
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows sustained growth as specified, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 45% of triggered instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of declining unemployment and strong retail sales indicates robust consumer health and confidence, translating into higher corporate revenues and earnings, which supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by > 0.2pp over 3 months AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.4%
evidence_refs: fact:consumer spending and employment are linked
failure_condition: If both UNRATE falls and RSAFS grows as specified, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate signals an accommodative monetary policy, reducing borrowing costs for corporations and encouraging investment, which supports equity valuations and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% for 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:low interest rates support asset prices
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% for 3 months, but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 45% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.
